privileged_info_summary: The task involves a synthetic dataset ('Chicago_WNV_Tests.csv') with 15 mosquito pool test records from August 2019. The data contains 7 positive records and 8 negative records for West Nile Virus testing. Both mosquito species mentioned—Culex pipiens and Culex restuans—are verified WNV vectors in Chicago, with Culex pipiens being the primary vector in Illinois. All geographic coordinates fall within realistic Chicago area bounds (latitude 41.64-41.99°N, longitude 87.53-87.89°W, centered near Chicago's actual center of 41.85°N, 87.65°W). August 2019 dates are appropriate for the official Chicago WNV surveillance season (June through October), during which the Chicago Department of Public Health's actual 2019 surveillance documented positive WNV pools. The filter condition 'RESULT = positive' correctly identifies 7 records for mapping. The expected output file path and filename (wnv_positive_map.png) match across all scripts.
